title: cymbopogon tortilis subsp. goeringii
intro: Cymbopogon tortilis subsp. goeringii, commonly known as East African lemon grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to East Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cymbopogon tortilis subsp. goeringii include relief from fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The plant can be prepared as an essential oil or herbal tea, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies.
